Okay, so the next thing I'm gonna show you is how you do a recurring order. So let's say that someone wants to get TestStorm on recurring. Okay, so we're gonna go to TestStorm, that's what they wanted, and you can click Subscribe and Save, and then you would click Add to Cart. Alright, this is a new session, we just completed the last transaction, and so this is a new person. We're going on there, and you'll notice that it's at 59 instead of 67, and that's because the Subscribe and Save discount was applied. In this instance, they will be charged $59 every month, every 30 days, and they will get shipped out a fresh bottle of TestStorm. Alright, there are no coupons that would be applicable for a one-off purchase, sorry for a single item purchase, not a one-off, but a single item purchase. At this point, you just confirm, this is a recurring total, right, it's going to total in the amount that they're going to be shipping. If you select down, the amount that they're shipping, the recurring charge will actually be slightly lower, but I will give you some words around why keeping it $8.99 is the way to do it. Okay, now proceed to checkout. Here it works exactly the same as the other one, alright, you're gonna fill out the details, and this is just making sure that they understand that this is a recurring transaction that is not a one-time thing, you'll enter the code, sorry, you'll enter the credit card information, replace the order, and the exact same thing will happen as what happened last time. Now, so that while I have your attention, well actually, that is the end of that video, and then I will show you how to do big packages.
